- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Post-run exhaustive forensic audit for POLARIS pipeline.
3	
4	Reads ALL trace events, cost ledger, result state, and final report to
5	produce a comprehensive 11-section forensic analysis. Every reasoning
6	stream, evidence piece, citation, and LLM call is examined line-by-line.
7	
8	11 Forensic Sections:
9	    1. Pipeline Timeline         Node durations, critical path, iteration rationale
10	    2. Planning Deep-Dive        Full reasoning text, query diversity analysis
11	    3. Search & Fetch Forensics  Every search/fetch, domain distribution, stub rate
12	    4. STORM Interview Review    Full Q&A transcripts, persona coverage
13	    5. Evidence Chain            Full funnel, every piece with quote text
14	    6. Verification Forensics    Every claim verdict, per-source faithfulness
15	    7. Report Text Forensics     Citation mapping, cross-section dedup, CoT scan
16	    8. Quality Gate Audit        Every gate with threshold vs actual
17	    9. LLM Call Audit            Every call with tokens/cost, cross-check ledger
18	   10. Anomaly Digest            Categorized counts from live anomaly log
19	   11. Benchmark Comparison      Run metrics vs competitor placeholders
20	
21	CLI: python scripts/forensic_audit.py --vector-id PG_TEST_059
22	     [--trace ...] [--result ...] [--report ...]
23	
24	Zero new dependencies. Standard library + json.
25	"""

73	def _load_jsonl(path: Path) -> list[dict]:
74	    """Load all lines from a JSONL file."""
75	    items = []
76	    if not path.exists():
77	        logger.warning("File not found: %s", path)
78	        return items
79	    with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
80	        for i, line in enumerate(f, 1):
81	            line = line.strip()
82	            if not line:
83	                continue
84	            try:
85	                items.append(json.loads(line))
86	            except json.JSONDecodeError:
87	                logger.debug("Malformed JSONL line %d in %s", i, path)
88	    return items

109	def _group_events(events: list[dict]) -> dict[str, list[dict]]:
110	    """Group trace events by type."""
111	    grouped: dict[str, list[dict]] = defaultdict(list)
112	    for ev in events:
113	        grouped[ev.get("type", "unknown")].append(ev)
114	    return grouped
115	
116	
117	def _filter_cost_ledger(
118	    all_entries: list[dict], trace_events: list[dict],
119	) -> list[dict]:
120	    """Filter cost ledger to entries within the trace's time window.
121	
122	    Uses the first and last trace event timestamps to bracket the run.
123	    Falls back to returning all entries if timestamps can't be parsed.
124	    """
125	    if not trace_events or not all_entries:
126	        return all_entries
127	
128	    # Extract time window from trace
129	    timestamps = [e.get("ts", "") for e in trace_events if e.get("ts")]
130	    if not timestamps:
131	        return all_entries
132	
133	    try:
134	        first_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(min(timestamps))
135	        last_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(max(timestamps))
136	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
137	        return all_entries
138	
139	    # Add 1-minute buffer on each side
140	    from datetime import timedelta
141	    start = first_ts - timedelta(minutes=1)
142	    end = last_ts + timedelta(minutes=1)
143	
144	    filtered = []
145	    for entry in all_entries:
146	        entry_ts_str = entry.get("timestamp", "")
147	        if not entry_ts_str:
148	            continue
149	        try:
150	            entry_ts = datetime.fromisoformat(entry_ts_str)
151	            if start <= entry_ts <= end:
152	                filtered.append(entry)
153	        except (ValueError, TypeError):
154	            continue
155	
156	    return filtered
157

159	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------

161	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
162	def _section_1_timeline(events: list[dict], grouped: dict) -> str:
163	    """Section 1: Pipeline Timeline."""
164	    lines = ["## 1. Pipeline Timeline\n"]
165	
166	    # Node start/end pairs
167	    node_starts = {}
168	    node_entries = []
169	    for ev in events:
170	        node = ev.get("node", "")
171	        if ev.get("type") == "node_start":
172	            node_starts[node] = ev.get("ts", "")
173	        elif ev.get("type") == "node_end":
174	            start_ts = node_starts.get(node, "")
175	            duration_ms = ev.get("duration_ms", 0)
176	            node_entries.append({
177	                "node": node,
178	                "start": start_ts,
179	                "end": ev.get("ts", ""),
180	                "duration_ms": duration_ms,
181	            })
182	
183	    if node_entries:
184	        lines.append("| Node | Start | End | Duration |")
185	        lines.append("|------|-------|-----|----------|")
186	        total_ms = 0
187	        for ne in node_entries:
188	            dur = ne["duration_ms"]
189	            total_ms += dur
190	            lines.append(
191	                f"| {ne['node']} | {_fmt_ts(ne['start'])} | "
192	                f"{_fmt_ts(ne['end'])} | {_fmt_dur(dur)} |"
193	            )
194	        lines.append(f"\n**Total pipeline time:** {_fmt_dur(total_ms)}\n")
195	
196	        # Critical path (longest node)
197	        if node_entries:
198	            longest = max(node_entries, key=lambda x: x["duration_ms"])
199	            lines.append(
200	                f"**Critical path:** {longest['node']} "
201	                f"({_fmt_dur(longest['duration_ms'])})\n"
202	            )
203	
204	    # Iteration decisions
205	    iter_decisions = grouped.get("iteration_decision", [])
206	    if iter_decisions:
207	        lines.append("### Iteration Decisions\n")
208	        for ev in iter_decisions:
209	            lines.append(f"**Iteration {ev.get('iteration', '?')}:** "
210	                         f"decision=`{ev.get('decision', '')}`\n")
211	            rationale = ev.get("rationale", {})
212	            if isinstance(rationale, dict):
213	                for k, v in rationale.items():
214	                    lines.append(f"- {k}: {v}")
215	            elif isinstance(rationale, str):
216	                lines.append(f"- {rationale}")
217	            lines.append("")
218	
219	    # Evaluate node enrichment (new visibility)
220	    evaluate_ends = [e for e in events if e.get("type") == "node_end" and e.get("node") == "evaluate"]
221	    for ev in evaluate_ends:
222	        delta = ev.get("evidence_delta")
223	        faith = ev.get("faithfulness")
224	        if delta is not None or faith is not None:
225	            lines.append("### Evaluate Node Details\n")
226	            if delta is not None:
227	                lines.append(f"- Evidence delta: {delta:+d}")
228	            if faith is not None:
229	                lines.append(f"- Faithfulness: {faith*100:.1f}%")
230	            lines.append("")
231	
232	    return "\n".join(lines)
233

235	def _section_2_planning(grouped: dict) -> str:
236	    """Section 2: Planning Deep-Dive."""
237	    lines = ["## 2. Planning Deep-Dive\n"]
238	
239	    # Reasoning captures for planning
240	    reasoning = [e for e in grouped.get("reasoning_capture", [])
241	                 if e.get("node") == "plan"]
242	    if reasoning:

251	            lines.append("```\n")
252	    else:
253	        lines.append("*No planning reasoning captured.*\n")
254	
255	    # Query generation
256	    queries = [e for e in grouped.get("query", []) if e.get("node") == "plan"]
257	    if queries:
258	        lines.append("### Query Generation\n")
259	        for ev in queries:
260	            lines.append(f"- {ev.get('action', '')}: {ev.get('count', 0)} queries")
261	        lines.append("")
262	
263	    # Query Plan analysis (new visibility emissions)
264	    plan_events = [e for e in grouped.get("evidence", [])
265	                   if e.get("action") in ("query_plan", "seed_query_plan")]
266	    if plan_events:
267	        lines.append("### Research Plan\n")
268	        for pe in plan_events:
269	            strategy = pe.get("search_strategy", "")
270	            concepts = pe.get("key_concepts", [])
271	            plan_queries = pe.get("queries", [])
272	            persp_dist = pe.get("perspective_distribution", {})
273	            missing = pe.get("missing_perspectives", [])
274	
275	            lines.append(f"**Search strategy:** {strategy}")
276	            if concepts:
277	                lines.append(f"**Key concepts:** {', '.join(concepts[:10])}")
278	            lines.append(f"**Queries generated:** {len(plan_queries)}\n")
279	
280	            if persp_dist:

282	                lines.append("|-------------|---------|")
283	                for p, cnt in sorted(persp_dist.items(), key=lambda x: -x[1]):
284	                    flag = " **MISSING**" if p in missing else ""
285	                    lines.append(f"| {p}{flag} | {cnt} |")
286	                lines.append("")
287	
288	            if missing:

290	
291	            if plan_queries:
292	                lines.append("| # | Query | Perspective | Intent |")
293	                lines.append("|---|-------|-------------|--------|")
294	                for i, q in enumerate(plan_queries[:30], 1):

296	                lines.append("")

298	    return "\n".join(lines)

332	
333	        # Academic vs web ratio

336	        acad_count = sum(len(evs) for eng, evs in by_engine.items()
337	                         if eng in ("semantic_scholar", "openalex"))
338	        total = web_count + acad_count
339	        if total > 0:
340	            lines.append(
341	                f"**Web/Academic ratio:** {web_count}/{acad_count} "
342	                f"({web_count/total:.0%}/{acad_count/total:.0%})\n"
343	            )
344	
345	    # Fetches
346	    fetches = grouped.get("fetch", [])
347	    if fetches:
348	        lines.append(f"### Fetch Results ({len(fetches)} total)\n")

350	        # Status distribution
351	        status_counts = Counter(e.get("status", "unknown") for e in fetches)

354	        for status, count in status_counts.most_common():
355	            pct = count / len(fetches) * 100
356	            lines.append(f"| {status} | {count} | {pct:.1f}% |")
357	        lines.append("")
358	
359	        # Domain distribution
360	        domains = Counter()
361	        for ev in fetches:
362	            url = ev.get("url", "")
363	            domain = _extract_domain(url)
364	            if domain:
365	                domains[domain] += 1
366	
367	        if domains:
368	            lines.append("### Domain Distribution (top 20)\n")
369	            lines.append("| Domain | Fetches | Avg Content (chars) |")
370	            lines.append("|--------|---------|---------------------|")
371	            domain_content = defaultdict(list)
372	            for ev in fetches:
373	                domain = _extract_domain(ev.get("url", ""))
374	                if domain:
375	                    domain_content[domain].append(ev.get("content_len", 0))
376	            for domain, count in domains.most_common(20):
377	                avg_len = sum(domain_content[domain]) / max(len(domain_content[domain]), 1)
378	                lines.append(f"| {domain} | {count} | {avg_len:.0f} |")
379	            lines.append("")
380	
381	        # Stub/paywall rate
382	        stub_count = sum(1 for e in fetches

384	        paywall_count = status_counts.get("paywall", 0) + status_counts.get("blocked", 0)

388	                f"**Paywall/blocked rate:** {paywall_count}/{len(fetches)} "

392	        # Duplicate URL list
393	        url_counts = Counter(e.get("url", "") for e in fetches)
394	        dupes = {url: cnt for url, cnt in url_counts.items() if cnt > 1 and url}
395	        if dupes:

400	
401	        # Full fetch log

403	        for ev in fetches:

405	            url = ev.get("url", "")
406	            clen = ev.get("content_len", 0)
407	            dur = ev.get("duration_ms", 0)

411	    # Agentic search rounds (new visibility emissions)

1205	def _fmt_ts(ts: str) -> str:
1206	    """Format ISO timestamp to HH:MM:SS."""
1207	    if not ts:
1208	        return "--"
1209	    try:
1210	        dt = datetime.fromisoformat(ts)
1211	        return dt.strftime("%H:%M:%S")
1212	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
1213	        return ts[:19]

1216	def _fmt_dur(ms) -> str:
1217	    """Format milliseconds as human-readable."""
1218	    if ms is None or ms == 0:
1219	        return "--"
1220	    ms = float(ms)
1221	    if ms < 1000:
1222	        return f"{ms:.0f}ms"
1223	    if ms < 60000:
1224	        return f"{ms / 1000:.1f}s"
1225	    return f"{ms / 60000:.1f}min"

1238	def _jaccard_words(text_a: str, text_b: str) -> float:
1239	    """Compute Jaccard similarity on word sets."""
1240	    if not text_a or not text_b:
1241	        return 0.0
1242	    words_a = set(text_a.lower().split())
1243	    words_b = set(text_b.lower().split())
1244	    if not words_a or not words_b:
1245	        return 0.0
1246	    intersection = words_a & words_b
1247	    union = words_a | words_b
1248	    return len(intersection) / len(union) if union else 0.0

1254	def run_forensic_audit(
1255	    vector_id: str,

1257	    result_path: Optional[Path] = None,
1258	    report_path: Optional[Path] = None,
1259	) -> tuple[str, dict]:
1260	    """Run the full 11-section forensic audit.
1261	
1262	    Returns (markdown_report, json_summary).
1263	    """
1264	    # Auto-discover files
1265	    if trace_path is None:
1266	        trace_path = Path(f"logs/pg_trace_{vector_id}.jsonl")
1267	    if result_path is None:
1268	        result_path = Path(f"outputs/polaris_graph/{vector_id}.json")

1297

1	"""Freeze the OFF-path ClassificationResult baseline for smoke S1 (GH #983).
2	
3	One-time generator. Enumerates the unique classified URLs across the
4	``outputs/**/live_corpus_dump.json`` corpus, reconstructs the
5	``ClassificationSignals`` each carries (url + title + domain), runs the OFF
6	(legacy-rule) path of ``classify_source_tier`` with PG_USE_AUTHORITY_MODEL
7	explicitly UNSET, and writes the deterministic baseline to
8	``tests/fixtures/authority/clinical_tier_baseline_off.json``.
9	
10	S1 then re-runs the same reconstruction and asserts byte-identity to this
11	baseline — proving the kill-switch OFF path did not drift when the dispatcher
12	+ additive fields landed. The baseline + the per-URL reconstructed signals are
13	committed so the offline replay is deterministic (no network).
14	
15	Usage:
16	    python scripts/freeze_clinical_tier_baseline.py
17	"""
18	from __future__ import annotations
19	
20	import glob
21	import json
22	import os
23	import sys
24	from pathlib import Path
25	
26	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
27	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
28	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
29	DUMP_GLOB = str(REPO_ROOT / "outputs" / "**" / "live_corpus_dump.json")
30	OUT_PATH = (
31	    REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "authority" / "clinical_tier_baseline_off.json"
32	)
33	
34	
35	def collect_unique_sources() -> list[dict[str, str]]:
36	    """Return the deduped {url, title, domain} list from every dump file."""
37	    by_url: dict[str, dict[str, str]] = {}
38	    for path in sorted(glob.glob(DUMP_GLOB, recursive=True)):
39	        try:
40	            rows = json.loads(Path(path).read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
41	        except (ValueError, OSError):
42	            continue
43	        if not isinstance(rows, list):
44	            continue
45	        for row in rows:
46	            if not isinstance(row, dict):
47	                continue
48	            url = row.get("url") or ""
49	            if not url or url in by_url:
50	                continue
51	            by_url[url] = {
52	                "url": url,
53	                "title": row.get("title", "") or "",
54	                "domain": row.get("domain", "") or "",
55	            }
56	    return [by_url[u] for u in sorted(by_url)]
57	
58	
59	def main() -> None:
60	    # The OFF path MUST be exercised — unset the flag explicitly.
61	    os.environ.pop("PG_USE_AUTHORITY_MODEL", None)
62	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import (
63	        ClassificationSignals,
64	        classify_source_tier,
65	    )
66	
67	    sources = collect_unique_sources()
68	    baseline: list[dict] = []
69	    for src in sources:
70	        signals = ClassificationSignals(url=src["url"], title=src["title"])
71	        result = classify_source_tier(signals)
72	        baseline.append(
73	            {
74	                "url": src["url"],
75	                "title": src["title"],
76	                "tier": result.tier.value,
77	                "confidence": result.confidence,
78	                "matched_rules": list(result.matched_rules),
79	                "reasons": list(result.reasons),
80	            }
81	        )
82	
83	    OUT_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
84	    OUT_PATH.write_text(
85	        json.dumps(baseline, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2), encoding="utf-8"
86	    )
87	    print(f"froze {len(baseline)} OFF-path results -> {OUT_PATH}")
88	    # Sanity echo so the freeze run is auditable.
89	    tiers: dict[str, int] = {}
90	    for row in baseline:
91	        tiers[row["tier"]] = tiers.get(row["tier"], 0) + 1
92	    print("tier distribution (OFF, url+title-only signals):", tiers)
93	
94	
95	if __name__ == "__main__":
96	    main()
97

2	"""
3	POLARIS Full Cycle: Preflight -> Purge -> Run -> Eval -> Audit
4	
5	FIX-224: Single authoritative entry point for pipeline execution.
6	Orchestrates the complete pipeline execution:
7	0. PREFLIGHT: Validate environment, feature flags, dependencies
8	1. PURGE: Clean all temp, state, outputs (optionally memory)
9	2. RUN: Execute full S1V1 pipeline
10	3. EVAL: Run RAGAS evaluation
11	4. AUDIT: Run comprehensive audit (v2 automated deep audit)
12	
13	Usage:
14	    python scripts/full_cycle.py                    # Run all phases
15	    python scripts/full_cycle.py --skip-purge       # Skip purge phase
16	    python scripts/full_cycle.py --wipe-memory      # Also wipe ChromaDB
17	    python scripts/full_cycle.py --dry-run           # Show what would happen
18	    python scripts/full_cycle.py --result-file <path> # Use existing result
19	"""
20	
21	import os
22	import sys
23	import json
24	import shutil
25	import logging
26	import argparse
27	import subprocess
28	from datetime import datetime
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	# Add project root to path
32	PROJECT_ROOT = Path(__file__).parent.parent
33	sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT))
34	os.chdir(PROJECT_ROOT)
35	
36	# Configure logging
37	log_file = f"logs/full_cycle_{datetime.now().strftime('%Y%m%d_%H%M%S')}.log"
38	logging.basicConfig(
39	    level=logging.INFO,
40	    format="%(asctime)s | %(levelname)-8s | %(message)s",
41	    handlers=[
42	        logging.StreamHandler(sys.stdout),
43	        logging.FileHandler(log_file)
44	    ]
45	)
46	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
47	
48	
49	def phase_banner(phase_num: int, phase_name: str, status: str = "STARTING"):
50	    """Print a phase banner."""
51	    logger.info("")
52	    logger.info("=" * 70)
53	    logger.info(f"PHASE {phase_num}: {phase_name} [{status}]")

57	def phase_0_preflight(dry_run: bool = False) -> bool:
58	    """Phase 0: Preflight Validation (FIX-224).

60	    Validates:
61	    - Required environment variables

63	    - Python dependencies
64	    - Scripts/preflight.py checks
65

68	    """
69	    phase_banner(0, "PREFLIGHT VALIDATION")
70	
71	    if dry_run:
72	        logger.info("[DRY RUN] Would validate environment and feature flags")
73	        phase_banner(0, "PREFLIGHT VALIDATION", "SKIPPED (dry-run)")

75	
76	    errors = []
77	
78	    # Check critical environment variables

88	    for var, desc in required_vars.items():

95	    # Check feature flags (FIX-214)

105	        else:
106	            logger.info(f"  {flag}={val} ({desc})")
107	
108	    # Check recommended flags

111	        "POLARIS_QUERY_TOPIC_ANCHOR": ("1", "Topic-anchored query simplification"),
112	    }
113	
114	    for flag, (expected, desc) in recommended_flags.items():

118	        else:

120	
121	    # Run scripts/preflight.py if available

140	
141	    if errors:

146	        phase_banner(0, "PREFLIGHT VALIDATION", "FAILED")

46	        if len(parts) >= 2 and len(parts[0]) == 40:

51	def _live_sha(file_path: pathlib.Path) -> str:
52	    if not file_path.exists():
53	        return ""
54	    try:
55	        result = subprocess.run(
56	            ["git", "-C", str(file_path.parent), "hash-object", file_path.name],
57	            capture_output=True,
58	            text=True,
59	            check=True,
60	            timeout=10,
61	        )
62	        return result.stdout.strip()
63	    except (subprocess.CalledProcessError, subprocess.TimeoutExpired, FileNotFoundError):
64	        return ""

66	
67	def _emit_deny(reason: str) -> None:
68	    payload = {
69	        "hookSpecificOutput": {
70	            "hookEventName": "PreToolUse",
71	            "permissionDecision": "deny",
72	            "permissionDecisionReason": reason,
73	        }
74	    }
75	    print(json.dumps(payload))
76	
77	
78	def main() -> int:
79	    repo_root = pathlib.Path(os.environ.get("CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR", os.getcwd()))
80	    today = datetime.date.today().strftime("%Y%m%d")
81	    stamp = repo_root / "state" / "polaris_restart" / f"session_started_{today}.stamp"
82	
83	    # iter 7 PRB6-P1-001 fix: ALWAYS verify SHAs on every tool call.
84	    # Earlier the hook auto-skipped if stamp existed → if CHARTER/PLAN changed
85	    # mid-day, the cage would not detect drift. Now verify every call; stamp is
86	    # informational only (records last successful check time + observed SHAs).
87	    pin_file = repo_root / "state" / "polaris_restart" / "charter_sha_pin.txt"
88	    pins = _read_pins(pin_file)
89	
90	    # polaris-controls location (PR-B2 relocation 2026-05-05 night per user
91	    # directive "everything inside polaris folder"). Primary: nested under
92	    # POLARIS repo root. Sibling fallback retained for fresh-clone-elsewhere
93	    # scenarios where someone unpacks to a layout that hasn't completed PR-B2.
94	    sister_candidates = [
95	        repo_root / "polaris-controls",
96	        repo_root.parent / "polaris-controls",
97	    ]
98	    sister_root = next((p for p in sister_candidates if p.exists()), sister_candidates[0])
99	
100	    drift = []
101	    live_shas = {}
102	    for fname in ("CHARTER.md", "PLAN.md"):
103	        live = _live_sha(sister_root / fname)
104	        live_shas[fname] = live
105	        pin = pins.get(fname, "")
106	        if not live or not pin or live != pin:
107	            drift.append(f"{fname} pin={pin or 'MISSING'} live={live or 'UNREADABLE'}")
108	
109	    if drift:
110	        _emit_deny(
111	            "SHA pin drift detected: " + "; ".join(drift) + ". "
112	            "Halt per Plan §10. Resolution requires user-side reconciliation: "
113	            "user reads polaris-controls/CHARTER.md + PLAN.md, decides whether "
114	            "the live SHAs are the new canonical, then signs a commit updating "
115	            "state/polaris_restart/charter_sha_pin.txt. Hook will allow tool calls "
116	            "again on next invocation after reconciliation. Claude must NOT write "
117	            "the stamp file directly."
118	        )
119	        return 0
120	
121	    active_file = repo_root / "state" / "active_issue.json"
122	    active = active_file.read_text(encoding="utf-8") if active_file.exists() else "{}"
123	
124	    stamp.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
125	    stamp.write_text(

127	        f"active_issue: {active}\n"
128	        f"charter_sha: {live_shas.get('CHARTER.md', '')}\n"
129	        f"plan_sha: {live_shas.get('PLAN.md', '')}\n",
130	        encoding="utf-8",
131	    )
132	    return 0

1	#!/usr/bin/env python
2	"""A18 — Hard release-invariant / conformance check (iarch007, Codex's #1 missing backstop).
3	
4	THE STRUCTURAL BACKSTOP (CI-enforced, not soft discipline) for the A2 seam rescue.
5	
6	The invariant (from `outputs/audits/iarch006_epic_failure/IMPROVED_ACTION_PLAN.md` A18):
7	
8	  EVERY run artifact that writes ``status == success`` / ``status ==
9	  released_with_disclosed_gaps`` / ``release_allowed == true`` MUST PROVE the
10	  four-role D8 judge actually adjudicated (a non-empty ``final_verdicts`` map, i.e.
11	  a real ``release_outcome``), OR PROVE the findings body is withheld / shipped only
12	  as an explicitly-disclosed seam gap. No findings prose may ship as
13	  verified/released unless the judge truly ran.
14	
15	This relaxes NOTHING. It is the same "structural removal, not promises" pattern as
16	the CHARTER: a path that mis-builds the A2 seam rescue (auto-releasing un-judged
17	content as ``success``) is caught here at the artifact layer, deterministically,
18	with a non-zero exit so CI fails the run.
19	
20	SCOPE (AGENT-NEWFILES, SAFE CORE): this checks the run ARTIFACTS
21	(``manifest.json`` / ``run_status.json``). The A18 "EVERY code path" reading also
22	wants a static scan of every site that writes a release status — that is fragile,
23	out of NEWFILES scope, and flagged for Codex. The artifact invariant is the binding
24	backstop a CI gate actually runs against a finished run directory.
25	
26	Usage::
27	
28	    python scripts/iarch007_release_invariant_check.py <run_dir_or_manifest> [...]
29	    python scripts/iarch007_release_invariant_check.py --self-test
30	
31	Exit code 0 == every artifact satisfies the invariant; non-zero == a violation
32	(an un-judged report marked success/released, or a release with no proof of D8 /
33	no withhold / no disclosed seam gap). The check is import-safe: nothing runs at
34	import time; all work is under functions + ``if __name__ == "__main__"``.
35	"""
36	
37	from __future__ import annotations
38	
39	import json
40	import sys
41	from pathlib import Path
42	from typing import Any
43	
44	# --- the release-status vocabulary this invariant binds (mirrors
45	# scripts/run_honest_sweep_r3.py UNIFIED_STATUS_VALUES + release_policy STATUS_*). These are
46	# read as plain strings here so the check has NO import dependency on the 10k-line sweep script
47	# (a CI gate must stay light + side-effect free). ----------------------------------------------
48	STATUS_SUCCESS = "success"
49	STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS = "released_with_disclosed_gaps"
50	STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY = "released_insufficient_safety_evidence"
51	
52	# Statuses that ASSERT a clean, judge-final report -> demand real D8 adjudication proof.
53	_STRICT_RELEASE_STATUSES = frozenset({STATUS_SUCCESS, STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS})
54	# Released-but-degraded statuses that ship an HONEST disclosed artifact -> demand a disclosed
55	# seam/insufficient gap OR a withheld body (never silent), but do NOT demand full D8 verdicts.
56	_DISCLOSED_RELEASE_STATUSES = frozenset(
57	    {STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS, STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY}
58	)
59	
60	# The disclosed-gap label the A2 seam rescue injects so an un-judged report can ONLY resolve to
61	# released_with_disclosed_gaps, never success (release_policy.py:569).
62	_SEAM_GAP_TOKEN = "four_role_seam_unadjudicated"
63	
64	
65	class ReleaseInvariantViolation(Exception):
66	    """Raised (collected) when an artifact violates the release invariant."""
67	
68	
69	def _coerce_bool(val: Any) -> bool:
70	    """Tolerant truthiness for a manifest field that may be a real bool or a string."""
71	    if isinstance(val, bool):
72	        return val
73	    if isinstance(val, str):
74	        return val.strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")
75	    return bool(val)
76	
77	
78	def _d8_adjudicated(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
79	    """Proof the four-role D8 judge ACTUALLY ran: a non-empty ``final_verdicts`` map.
80	
81	    The sweep writes ``manifest['four_role_evaluation']['final_verdicts']`` (claim_id ->
82	    verdict). An EMPTY map is the exact seam-error signature (drb_90:
83	    abort_four_role_release_held with final_verdicts={}). A non-empty map proves the judge
84	    adjudicated at least one claim.
85	    """
86	    fr = manifest.get("four_role_evaluation")
87	    if not isinstance(fr, dict):
88	        return False
89	    fv = fr.get("final_verdicts")
90	    return isinstance(fv, dict) and len(fv) > 0
91	
92	
93	def _disclosed_gaps_list(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
94	    """The disclosed-gap labels, as strings, from release_disclosure (or the top-level fallback)."""
95	    rd = manifest.get("release_disclosure")
96	    gaps: Any = None
97	    if isinstance(rd, dict):
98	        gaps = rd.get("disclosed_gaps")
99	    if not isinstance(gaps, list) or not gaps:
100	        # top-level disclosed_gaps is the runner's seam-side fallback channel.
101	        gaps = manifest.get("disclosed_gaps")
102	    if not isinstance(gaps, list):
103	        return []
104	    return [str(g) for g in gaps]
105	
106	
107	def _seam_gap_present(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
108	    """The SPECIFIC four_role_seam_unadjudicated label is present (iarch007 SWEEP-P0 tightening).
109	
110	    The pre-fix check accepted ANY non-empty disclosed_gaps list as seam proof — a bypass: a
111	    manifest with an UNRELATED gap (a credibility-unscored label, say) and no real seam token
112	    passed. The A2 seam rescue ALWAYS injects the explicit ``four_role_seam_unadjudicated`` token
113	    (release_policy.py self-injects it defence-in-depth), so the seam disposition is proven ONLY by
114	    that specific label, never by an arbitrary gap.
115	    """
116	    return any(_SEAM_GAP_TOKEN in g for g in _disclosed_gaps_list(manifest))
117	
118	
119	def _safety_floor_gap_present(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
120	    """The insufficient-safety-floor disclosure label is present (the legit label for the
121	    released_insufficient_safety_evidence terminal — see self-test case (e))."""
122	    return any("safety_floor_insufficient" in g for g in _disclosed_gaps_list(manifest))
123	
124	
125	def _body_withheld(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
126	    """Proof the findings body was WITHHELD — read STRUCTURALLY (iarch007 SWEEP-P0 tightening).
127	
128	    The pre-fix check treated ``normal_release_blocked`` as body-withheld proof. That is WRONG:
129	    ``normal_release_blocked`` only means the polished NORMAL render was suppressed (e.g. an
130	    insufficient-safety variant still SHIPS a findings body) — it does NOT prove the findings body
131	    itself was withheld. Read the real serialized ``body_withheld`` flag (set by the A2 seam
132	    rescue / compute_release_outcome) instead; the top-level ``body_withheld`` is a legacy fallback.
133	    """
134	    rd = manifest.get("release_disclosure")
135	    if isinstance(rd, dict) and _coerce_bool(rd.get("body_withheld")):
136	        return True
137	    return _coerce_bool(manifest.get("body_withheld"))
138	
139	
140	def _compensating_screen_passed(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> bool:
141	    """Proof a compensating standalone fabrication screen RAN CLEAN on a body-shipping seam."""
142	    rd = manifest.get("release_disclosure")
143	    return isinstance(rd, dict) and _coerce_bool(rd.get("compensating_screen_passed"))
144	
145	
146	def check_manifest(manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> list[str]:
147	    """Return a list of invariant-violation messages for ONE manifest dict (empty == OK).
148	
149	    The invariant (A18):
150	      1. ``status == success`` (the strongest assertion: a clean judge-final report) requires
151	         REAL D8 adjudication (non-empty final_verdicts). A success with no D8 proof is the
152	         catastrophic path A2 must never reach.
153	      2. ``status == released_with_disclosed_gaps`` is RELEASED-with-disclosure: it requires
154	         EITHER real D8 adjudication OR a non-empty disclosed-gap disclosure (the honest seam
155	         rescue) OR a withheld body. It may NOT ship with zero D8 proof AND zero disclosure.
156	      3. ``status == released_insufficient_safety_evidence`` requires a non-empty disclosure
157	         OR a withheld body (it is by definition a degraded, disclosed artifact).
158	      4. ``release_allowed == true`` with a NON-release/abort status is a contradiction
159	         (the binding decision says ship, the status says abort) -> violation.
160	      5. An ``abort_*`` status with ``release_allowed == false`` ALWAYS satisfies the invariant
161	         (nothing shipped) — this is the correct fail-closed disposition (drb_90's held run).
162	    """
163	    violations: list[str] = []
164	    status = str(manifest.get("status") or "")
165	    release_allowed = _coerce_bool(manifest.get("release_allowed"))
166	    d8_ran = _d8_adjudicated(manifest)
167	    seam_gap = _seam_gap_present(manifest)
168	    safety_gap = _safety_floor_gap_present(manifest)
169	    withheld = _body_withheld(manifest)
170	    screen_passed = _compensating_screen_passed(manifest)
171	
172	    # The seam-rescue proof (the ONLY way a released_with_disclosed_gaps may ship un-judged): the
173	    # SPECIFIC four_role_seam_unadjudicated label is present AND the body is provably safe — either
174	    # withheld OR a compensating fabrication screen ran clean. An arbitrary disclosed gap is NOT
175	    # proof (iarch007 SWEEP-P0: the pre-fix `any non-empty gap` accept was the bypass).
176	    seam_rescue_proven = seam_gap and (withheld or screen_passed)
177	
178	    # (1) success demands real D8 adjudication — no exceptions. This is the A2 catastrophe guard.
179	    if status == STATUS_SUCCESS and not d8_ran:
180	        violations.append(
181	            "status=success but four_role D8 never adjudicated (final_verdicts empty): an "
182	            "un-judged report marked SUCCESS. The A2 seam rescue must resolve to "
183	            "released_with_disclosed_gaps (never success) on a judge seam-error."
184	        )
185	
186	    # (2) released_with_disclosed_gaps: real D8 ran OR the seam rescue is PROVEN (specific seam
187	    # token + withheld-or-screened body). A bare non-empty disclosed_gaps no longer counts.
188	    if status == STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS and not (d8_ran or seam_rescue_proven):
189	        violations.append(
190	            "status=released_with_disclosed_gaps but there is NO D8 adjudication and NO PROVEN "
191	            "seam rescue (the specific four_role_seam_unadjudicated label PLUS a withheld body OR "
192	            "a passed compensating fabrication screen). A released report with no real judging and "
193	            "no proven-safe seam disposition is a silent un-judged release."
194	        )
195	
196	    # (3) released_insufficient_safety_evidence: this degraded terminal must carry its HONEST
197	    # safety-floor disclosure OR a withheld body OR a proven seam rescue. A bare unrelated gap is
198	    # not sufficient — the specific safety_floor_insufficient label (or a withheld body) proves it.
199	    if status == STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY and not (
200	        safety_gap or withheld or seam_rescue_proven
201	    ):
202	        violations.append(
203	            "status=released_insufficient_safety_evidence but no safety_floor_insufficient "
204	            "disclosure, no withheld body, and no proven seam rescue — the insufficient-safety "
205	            "variant must ship its honest safety-floor disclosure."
206	        )
207	
208	    # (4) release_allowed true with a non-release/abort status is a contradiction.
209	    if release_allowed and status and status not in (
210	        _STRICT_RELEASE_STATUSES | _DISCLOSED_RELEASE_STATUSES
211	    ):
212	        # partial_* statuses are a known shippable-degraded family; only abort_* / unknown are a
213	        # contradiction with release_allowed=true.
214	        if status.startswith("abort"):
215	            violations.append(
216	                f"release_allowed=true but status={status!r} (an abort): the binding release "
217	                "decision contradicts the terminal status."
218	            )
219	
220	    # (5) release_allowed true on a strict-release status still demands the D8 proof OR a PROVEN
221	    # seam rescue — a true release_allowed with no judging and no proven-safe seam is the same
222	    # silent-release hole as (2).
223	    if release_allowed and status in _STRICT_RELEASE_STATUSES and not (
224	        d8_ran or seam_rescue_proven
225	    ):
226	        violations.append(
227	            f"release_allowed=true on status={status!r} with no D8 adjudication and no proven "
228	            "seam rescue — release without proof the judge ran or the seam body is safe."
229	        )
230	
231	    # (6) release_allowed=true on ANY OTHER non-abort status (partial_*/unknown/unclassified) —
232	    # one that is NEITHER a strict-release status (covered by (5)) NOR a disclosed-release status
233	    # (covered by (2)/(3) with their own seam/safety allowances) — STILL demands real D8 proof OR a
234	    # PROVEN seam rescue. The invariant's own rule (release_allowed==true must prove D8 or a safe
235	    # seam disposition) applies to EVERY release, not only the strict family. Pre-fix, these
236	    # statuses slipped through entirely: e.g. status="partial_saturation", release_allowed=true,
237	    # final_verdicts={} passed with no judging — the silent-release hole Codex (iarch007 SWEEP-P0)
238	    # caught. Faithfulness is STRENGTHENED, never relaxed: more release shapes must now prove safety.
239	    if (
240	        release_allowed
241	        and status
242	        and not status.startswith("abort")
243	        and status not in _STRICT_RELEASE_STATUSES
244	        and status not in _DISCLOSED_RELEASE_STATUSES
245	        and not (d8_ran or seam_rescue_proven)
246	    ):
247	        violations.append(
248	            f"release_allowed=true on non-abort status={status!r} (neither a strict nor a "
249	            "disclosed release status) with no D8 adjudication and no proven seam rescue — a "
250	            "release with no proof the judge ran or the seam body is safe "
251	            "(the partial_*/unknown silent-release hole)."
252	        )
253	
254	    return violations
255	
256	
257	def _load_manifest(path: Path) -> dict[str, Any]:
258	    """Load a manifest dict from a file path. Fails loudly (LAW II) on a missing/garbage file."""
259	    if not path.is_file():
260	        raise FileNotFoundError(f"release-invariant: manifest not found: {path}")
261	    with path.open(encoding="utf-8") as handle:
262	        data = json.load(handle)
263	    if not isinstance(data, dict):
264	        raise ValueError(f"release-invariant: manifest is not a JSON object: {path}")
265	    return data
266	
267	
268	def resolve_manifest_paths(target: Path) -> list[Path]:
269	    """Resolve a CLI target to the manifest file(s) it names.
270	
271	    A file path is used directly. A directory is searched for ``manifest.json`` then
272	    ``run_status.json`` (the two artifacts that carry a binding status). Both are checked when
273	    present so a status that disagrees between them is also caught.
274	    """
275	    if target.is_file():
276	        return [target]
277	    if target.is_dir():
278	        found = [target / name for name in ("manifest.json", "run_status.json")]
279	        present = [p for p in found if p.is_file()]
280	        if not present:
281	            raise FileNotFoundError(
282	                f"release-invariant: no manifest.json/run_status.json under {target}"
283	            )
284	        return present
285	    raise FileNotFoundError(f"release-invariant: target not found: {target}")
286	
287	
288	def check_targets(targets: list[str]) -> tuple[int, list[str]]:
289	    """Check every target; return (violation_count, messages). Pure — no exit, no print."""
290	    all_messages: list[str] = []
291	    for raw in targets:
292	        target = Path(raw)
293	        for manifest_path in resolve_manifest_paths(target):
294	            manifest = _load_manifest(manifest_path)
295	            for msg in check_manifest(manifest):
296	                all_messages.append(f"{manifest_path}: {msg}")
297	    return len(all_messages), all_messages
298	
299	
300	# --- self-test (no network, no run dir needed): synthetic + the real drb_90 held manifest ------
301	def _self_test() -> int:
302	    """Deterministic offline self-test of the invariant logic. Returns a process exit code."""
303	    failures: list[str] = []
304	
305	    def expect(name: str, manifest: dict[str, Any], should_violate: bool) -> None:
306	        v = check_manifest(manifest)
307	        violated = bool(v)
308	        if violated != should_violate:
309	            failures.append(
310	                f"{name}: expected violate={should_violate}, got {violated} ({v})"
311	            )
312	
313	    # (a) success WITHOUT D8 verdicts -> VIOLATION (the A2 catastrophe).
314	    expect(
315	        "success_no_d8",
316	        {"status": STATUS_SUCCESS, "release_allowed": True,
317	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {}}},
318	        should_violate=True,
319	    )
320	    # (b) success WITH D8 verdicts -> OK.
321	    expect(
322	        "success_with_d8",
323	        {"status": STATUS_SUCCESS, "release_allowed": True,
324	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {"c1": "VERIFIED"}}},
325	        should_violate=False,
326	    )
327	    # (c) released_with_disclosed_gaps via PROVEN seam rescue (specific seam token + a passed
328	    # compensating fabrication screen, no D8) -> OK (the honest A2 rescue, body ships screened).
329	    expect(
330	        "disclosed_gaps_seam_rescue_screen_passed",
331	        {"status": STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS, "release_allowed": True,
332	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {}},
333	         "release_disclosure": {
334	             "disclosed_gaps": [f"{_SEAM_GAP_TOKEN}: judge unreachable"],
335	             "adjudicated": False, "body_withheld": False,
336	             "compensating_screen_passed": True}},
337	        should_violate=False,
338	    )
339	    # (c2) released_with_disclosed_gaps via seam rescue with a WITHHELD body (screen could not run)
340	    # -> OK (the Codex floor: disclosure ships, no findings prose).
341	    expect(
342	        "disclosed_gaps_seam_rescue_body_withheld",
343	        {"status": STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS, "release_allowed": True,
344	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {}},
345	         "release_disclosure": {
346	             "disclosed_gaps": [f"{_SEAM_GAP_TOKEN}: judge unreachable"],
347	             "adjudicated": False, "body_withheld": True,
348	             "compensating_screen_passed": False}},
349	        should_violate=False,
350	    )
351	    # (c3) released_with_disclosed_gaps carrying the seam token but NO proof (no withheld body, no
352	    # passed screen) -> VIOLATION (the seam body ships un-judged AND un-screened: the leak A18 closes).
353	    expect(
354	        "disclosed_gaps_seam_token_but_no_proof",
355	        {"status": STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS, "release_allowed": True,
356	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {}},
357	         "release_disclosure": {
358	             "disclosed_gaps": [f"{_SEAM_GAP_TOKEN}: judge unreachable"],
359	             "adjudicated": False, "body_withheld": False,
360	             "compensating_screen_passed": False}},
361	        should_violate=True,
362	    )
363	    # (c4) released_with_disclosed_gaps with an ARBITRARY (non-seam) gap and no D8 -> VIOLATION
364	    # (iarch007 SWEEP-P0: a bare non-empty disclosed_gaps list is NO LONGER seam proof).
365	    expect(
366	        "disclosed_gaps_arbitrary_gap_no_seam_token",
367	        {"status": STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS, "release_allowed": True,
368	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {}},
369	         "release_disclosure": {
370	             "disclosed_gaps": ["credibility_unscored: 3 sources at neutral weight"],
371	             "adjudicated": False, "body_withheld": False,
372	             "compensating_screen_passed": False}},
373	        should_violate=True,
374	    )
375	    # (d) released_with_disclosed_gaps with NO disclosure and NO D8 -> VIOLATION (silent release).
376	    expect(
377	        "disclosed_gaps_no_disclosure",
378	        {"status": STATUS_RELEASED_WITH_DISCLOSED_GAPS, "release_allowed": True,
379	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {}}},
380	        should_violate=True,
381	    )
382	    # (e) insufficient-safety with the SPECIFIC safety-floor disclosure -> OK (the honest variant).
383	    expect(
384	        "insufficient_safety_disclosed",
385	        {"status": STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY, "release_allowed": True,
386	         "release_disclosure": {"disclosed_gaps": ["safety_floor_insufficient: ..."],
387	                                "adjudicated": False, "normal_release_blocked": True}},
388	        should_violate=False,
389	    )
390	    # (e2) insufficient-safety with ONLY normal_release_blocked (no safety-floor label, no withheld
391	    # body) -> VIOLATION (iarch007 SWEEP-P0: normal_release_blocked is NOT body-withheld proof).
392	    expect(
393	        "insufficient_safety_only_normal_render_blocked",
394	        {"status": STATUS_RELEASED_INSUFFICIENT_SAFETY, "release_allowed": True,
395	         "release_disclosure": {"disclosed_gaps": ["some_unrelated_label"],
396	                                "normal_release_blocked": True}},
397	        should_violate=True,
398	    )
399	    # (f) abort with release_allowed False -> OK (the correct fail-closed disposition).
400	    expect(
401	        "abort_held_failclosed",
402	        {"status": "abort_four_role_release_held", "release_allowed": False,
403	         "four_role_evaluation": {"final_verdicts": {}}},
404	        should_violate=False,
405	    )
406	    # (g) release_allowed True on an abort status -> VIOLATION (contradiction).
407	    expect(
408	        "release_allowed_on_abort",
409	        {"status": "abort_no_verified_sections", "release_allowed": True},
410	        should_violate=True,
411	    )
412	
413	    # (h) the REAL drb_90 held run (box_pull): abort_four_role_release_held, release_allowed False,
414	    # final_verdicts empty -> the invariant must PASS it (nothing shipped — correct fail-closed).
415	    real_manifest = (
416	        Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
417	        / "tests" / "fixtures" / "drb90_redaction" / "manifest.json"
418	    )
419	    if real_manifest.is_file():
420	        m = _load_manifest(real_manifest)
421	        v = check_manifest(m)
422	        if v:
423	            failures.append(f"real_drb90_manifest: expected NO violation, got {v}")
424	    else:
425	        print(f"[self-test] WARN: real drb_90 fixture not found at {real_manifest}")
426	
427	    if failures:
428	        print("RELEASE-INVARIANT SELF-TEST: FAIL")
429	        for f in failures:
430	            print(f"  - {f}")
431	        return 1
432	    print(
433	        "RELEASE-INVARIANT SELF-TEST: PASS (13 cases incl. the real drb_90 held manifest + the "
434	        "iarch007 SWEEP-P0 seam-proof tightening)"
435	    )
436	    return 0
437	
438	
439	def main(argv: list[str]) -> int:
440	    args = argv[1:]
441	    if not args or args[0] in ("-h", "--help"):
442	        print(__doc__)
443	        return 0
444	    if args[0] == "--self-test":
445	        return _self_test()
446	    try:
447	        count, messages = check_targets(args)
448	    except (FileNotFoundError, ValueError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
449	        print(f"RELEASE-INVARIANT: ERROR — {exc}", file=sys.stderr)
450	        return 2
451	    if count:
452	        print(f"RELEASE-INVARIANT: {count} VIOLATION(S) — release path is UNSOUND:")
453	        for msg in messages:
454	            print(f"  - {msg}")
455	        return 1
456	    print(f"RELEASE-INVARIANT: OK — {len(args)} target(s) satisfy the no-unjudged-release gate.")
457	    return 0
458	
459	
460	if __name__ == "__main__":
461	    sys.exit(main(sys.argv))
462

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""I-arch-010 PR1a behavioral replay-harness — the NO-LEAK proof (§-1.4, FAIL LOUD).
3	
4	Acceptance for PR1a is BEHAVIORAL: the no-leak effect must ACTUALLY APPEAR in the real
5	classification + assembly output, not "Codex approved the diff" and not "tests are green"
6	(CLAUDE.md §-1.4). This harness drives the PRODUCTION classifier + the REAL basket
7	assembly (``credibility_pass._assemble_baskets``) through a deterministic fake
8	``verify_fn`` (the same ``verify_fn`` seam ``_verify_member_in_isolation`` injects), and
9	FAILS LOUD (non-zero exit) if any case's effect did not fire.
10	
11	Injection mechanism (per the design plan): the fake ``verify_fn`` returns a
12	``SentenceVerification``-shaped result with a CHOSEN ``is_verified`` / ``judge_error`` /
13	``failure_reasons`` for each evidence_id. That result flows the REAL path
14	``verify_fn -> _verify_member_in_isolation -> _classify_member_tier`` AND the REAL
15	``_assemble_baskets`` reassembly, so the harness exercises the production classification +
16	no-leak count logic, NOT a mock of it.
17	
18	Codex-REQUIRED cases:
19	  * CASE A (P1-1 deterministic-NEUTRAL): a member that PASSES the deterministic (a)-(e)
20	    engine but whose entailment verdict is NEUTRAL -> NOT counted in
21	    ``verified_support_origin_count``, NOT render-eligible (``span_verdict == "UNSUPPORTED"``),
22	    ``member_tier == "DETERMINISTIC_ONLY"`` (grounded-but-weak, I-arch-011-surfaceable).
23	  * CASE B (P1-1 judge_error / Leak-1): a member whose entailment judge ERRORS (transport
24	    sentinel) while ``is_verified == True`` (FIX-1 keeps it on the deterministic checks) ->
25	    NOT counted, NOT render-eligible (``span_verdict == "UNSUPPORTED"``),
26	    ``member_tier == "DETERMINISTIC_ONLY"``. The Leak-1 closure proof: the ``judge_error``
27	    BOOL, not ``is_verified``, gates the count.
28	  * CASE 4b (tier-distinction): a member whose OWN span FAILS the deterministic (a)-(e)
29	    engine -> ``member_tier == "UNVERIFIED"`` (DISTINCT from DETERMINISTIC_ONLY) so
30	    I-arch-011 never surfaces deterministic garbage as a weak candidate.
31	  * CASE positive (non-vacuous control): a genuinely ENTAILED member (is_verified, no
32	    judge_error) -> COUNTED, render-eligible (``span_verdict == "SUPPORTS"``),
33	    ``member_tier == "ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED"``. Proves the fixture CAN count a member, so the
34	    A/B/4b exclusions are real.
35	
36	Run: ``python scripts/iarch010_replay_breadth_faithfulness_harness.py`` -> exit 0 if every
37	case fires, non-zero + the failing case/member on any miss.
38	"""
39	from __future__ import annotations
40	
41	import sys
42	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
43	from pathlib import Path
44	from types import SimpleNamespace
45	
46	# Make the repo root importable so the harness runs standalone (§-1.4: run directly, not
47	# only under pytest). scripts/ is one level under the repo root.
48	_REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent
49	if str(_REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
50	    sys.path.insert(0, str(_REPO_ROOT))
51	
52	from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import _basket_for_biblio
53	from src.polaris_graph.synthesis.credibility_pass import (
54	    MEMBER_TIER_DETERMINISTIC_ONLY,
55	    MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED,
56	    MEMBER_TIER_UNVERIFIED,
57	    _assemble_baskets,
58	)
59	
60	# A span that deterministically PASSES (a)-(e) for the positive/A/B members and a claim
61	# text whose number + content words appear in it. The DETERMINISTIC pass/fail is driven by
62	# the fake verify_fn below, so these strings only need to be non-empty + stable.
63	_GOOD_SPAN = "Tirzepatide lowered HbA1c by 2.3 percentage points in the SURPASS trial."
64	_GOOD_CLAIM = "Tirzepatide lowered HbA1c by 2.3 points."
65	
66	
67	@dataclass
68	class _FakeSentenceVerification:
69	    """The minimal shape ``_classify_member_tier`` reads off the verify result."""
70	
71	    is_verified: bool
72	    judge_error: bool = False
73	    failure_reasons: list = field(default_factory=list)
74	
75	
76	# evidence_id -> the verify result the fake verify_fn returns for that member. This is the
77	# ONLY place each case's verdict is chosen; everything downstream is production code.
78	_VERDICT_BY_EID: dict[str, _FakeSentenceVerification] = {
79	    # CASE positive — genuine ENTAILED: passes (a)-(e), judge ran clean.
80	    "ev_positive": _FakeSentenceVerification(is_verified=True, judge_error=False),
81	    # CASE A — deterministic-PASS but entailment NEUTRAL: is_verified=False with ONLY an
82	    # entailment_failed failure reason (the (a)-(e) engine passed; only the judge dropped).
83	    "ev_case_a": _FakeSentenceVerification(
84	        is_verified=False,
85	        judge_error=False,
86	        failure_reasons=["entailment_failed:ev_case_a:verdict=NEUTRAL:reason=injected"],
87	    ),
88	    # CASE B — judge_error / Leak-1: is_verified=True (FIX-1 keeps it on (a)-(e)) AND
89	    # judge_error=True (the transport sentinel). Must NOT count/render.
90	    "ev_case_b": _FakeSentenceVerification(is_verified=True, judge_error=True),
91	    # CASE 4b — deterministic GARBAGE: is_verified=False with a NON-entailment failure
92	    # reason (the member's own span fails (a)-(e): missing number / content-overlap).
93	    "ev_case_4b": _FakeSentenceVerification(
94	        is_verified=False,
95	        judge_error=False,
96	        failure_reasons=["content_overlap_below_min:ev_case_4b"],
97	    ),
98	}
99	
100	
101	def _fake_verify_fn(sentence: str, pool: dict):
102	    """The injected verifier: returns the chosen result for the single pooled member.
103	
104	    ``_verify_member_in_isolation`` builds a one-token sentence + a single-member pool, so
105	    ``pool`` has exactly one evidence_id — we key the chosen verdict off it.
106	    """
107	    eids = list(pool.keys())
108	    if len(eids) != 1:
109	        raise AssertionError(
110	            f"harness invariant broken: isolation pool must have exactly 1 member, got {eids!r}"
111	        )
112	    eid = eids[0]
113	    if eid not in _VERDICT_BY_EID:
114	        raise AssertionError(f"harness has no chosen verdict for evidence_id {eid!r}")
115	    return _VERDICT_BY_EID[eid]
116	
117	
118	def _build_inputs():
119	    """One claim cluster ``c_case`` with FOUR members (positive / A / B / 4b), each its own
120	    origin so a counted member increments ``verified_support_origin_count`` distinctly.
121	
122	    Returns ``(graph, weight_mass, annotated, credibility_by_evidence)`` in the shape
123	    ``_assemble_baskets`` consumes (duck-typed SimpleNamespace claims/graph)."""
124	    eids = ["ev_positive", "ev_case_a", "ev_case_b", "ev_case_4b"]
125	    # Each claim is a duck-typed object with .text / .evidence_id / .source_url / .source_tier.
126	    claims = [
127	        SimpleNamespace(
128	            text=_GOOD_CLAIM,
129	            evidence_id=eid,
130	            source_url=f"https://{eid}/",
131	            source_tier="T1",
132	        )
133	        for eid in eids
134	    ]
135	    # graph: .clusters maps cluster_id -> [claim indices]; .claims is the flat list;
136	    # .edges is empty (no refuters).
137	    graph = SimpleNamespace(
138	        clusters={"c_case": list(range(len(eids)))},
139	        claims=claims,
140	        edges=[],
141	    )
142	    # weight_mass: one ClaimWeightMass-shaped object per cluster (advisory count only).
143	    weight_mass = [
144	        SimpleNamespace(claim_cluster_id="c_case", independent_origin_count=len(eids))
145	    ]
146	    # annotated rows: each carries the member's own span (direct_quote) the verifier reads.
147	    annotated = [
148	        {"evidence_id": eid, "direct_quote": _GOOD_SPAN, "authority_score": 0.9}
149	        for eid in eids
150	    ]
151	    # credibility_by_evidence: each member maps to its OWN distinct origin cluster so a
152	    # counted member is a distinct verified origin.
153	    credibility_by_evidence = {
154	        eid: SimpleNamespace(origin_cluster_id=f"o::{eid}", credibility_weight=0.9)
155	        for eid in eids
156	    }
157	    return graph, weight_mass, annotated, credibility_by_evidence
158	
159	
160	def _fail(case: str, detail: str) -> None:
161	    print(f"FAIL [{case}]: {detail}", file=sys.stderr)
162	    sys.exit(1)
163	
164	
165	def main() -> int:
166	    graph, weight_mass, annotated, cred = _build_inputs()
167	    baskets = _assemble_baskets(
168	        graph, weight_mass, annotated, cred, verify_fn=_fake_verify_fn, max_inflight=1,
169	    )
170	    if len(baskets) != 1:
171	        _fail("setup", f"expected exactly 1 basket, got {len(baskets)}")
172	    basket = baskets[0]
173	    members = {m.evidence_id: m for m in basket.supporting_members}
174	    expected_eids = {"ev_positive", "ev_case_a", "ev_case_b", "ev_case_4b"}
175	    if set(members) != expected_eids:
176	        _fail("setup", f"member eids {set(members)!r} != expected {expected_eids!r}")
177	
178	    # ── per-member tier + span_verdict assertions (the production classifier output) ──
179	    expected = {
180	        "ev_positive": ("SUPPORTS", MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED),
181	        "ev_case_a": ("UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_DETERMINISTIC_ONLY),
182	        "ev_case_b": ("UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_DETERMINISTIC_ONLY),
183	        "ev_case_4b": ("UNSUPPORTED", MEMBER_TIER_UNVERIFIED),
184	    }
185	    for eid, (want_sv, want_tier) in expected.items():
186	        m = members[eid]
187	        if m.span_verdict != want_sv:
188	            _fail(eid, f"span_verdict {m.span_verdict!r} != expected {want_sv!r}")
189	        if m.member_tier != want_tier:
190	            _fail(eid, f"member_tier {m.member_tier!r} != expected {want_tier!r}")
191	
192	    # ── binding invariant: span_verdict=="SUPPORTS" IFF member_tier==ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED ──
193	    for eid, m in members.items():
194	        sv_supports = m.span_verdict == "SUPPORTS"
195	        tier_entailed = m.member_tier == MEMBER_TIER_ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED
196	        if sv_supports != tier_entailed:
197	            _fail(
198	                eid,
199	                "binding invariant broken: span_verdict=='SUPPORTS' "
200	                f"({sv_supports}) != member_tier=='ENTAILMENT_VERIFIED' ({tier_entailed})",
201	            )
202	
203	    # ── NO-LEAK assembly proof: only the genuine ENTAILED member is counted ──
204	    # verified_support_origin_count counts DISTINCT verified origins; exactly one member
205	    # (ev_positive) is genuine ENTAILED, so it must be 1 — A/B/4b leaked NOTHING.
206	    if basket.verified_support_origin_count != 1:
207	        _fail(
208	            "no_leak_count",
209	            "verified_support_origin_count must be 1 (only ev_positive genuine ENTAILED); "
210	            f"got {basket.verified_support_origin_count} — a non-entailed member LEAKED into the count",
211	        )
212	
213	    # ── render-eligibility proof via the biblio projection (the render/count consumers) ──
214	    # build_basket_supports_by_cluster keys on span_verdict=="SUPPORTS"; the projection
215	    # carries both span_verdict and member_tier. Assert ONLY ev_positive is render-eligible
216	    # and member_tier is faithfully projected for the I-arch-011 seam.
217	    proj = _basket_for_biblio(basket)
218	    proj_members = {mm["evidence_id"]: mm for mm in proj["supporting_members"]}
219	    render_eligible = {
220	        eid for eid, mm in proj_members.items()
221	        if str(mm.get("span_verdict") or "").upper() == "SUPPORTS"
222	    }
223	    if render_eligible != {"ev_positive"}:
224	        _fail(
225	            "no_leak_render",
226	            f"render-eligible (span_verdict==SUPPORTS) members {render_eligible!r} "
227	            "!= {'ev_positive'} — a non-entailed member is render-eligible (LEAK)",
228	        )
229	    for eid, (_, want_tier) in expected.items():
230	        got_tier = str(proj_members[eid].get("member_tier") or "")
231	        if got_tier != want_tier:
232	            _fail(
233	                "biblio_projection",
234	                f"{eid}: projected member_tier {got_tier!r} != expected {want_tier!r} "
235	                "(I-arch-011 seam not carried through the projection)",
236	            )
237	
238	    print(
239	        "PASS iarch010 PR1a no-leak harness: "
240	        "CASE A (deterministic-NEUTRAL) NOT counted/rendered, member_tier=DETERMINISTIC_ONLY; "
241	        "CASE B (judge_error) NOT counted/rendered, member_tier=DETERMINISTIC_ONLY; "
242	        "CASE 4b (deterministic-garbage) member_tier=UNVERIFIED (distinct); "
243	        "positive ENTAILED counted=1, render-eligible={ev_positive}; "
244	        "binding invariant held; biblio projection carries member_tier."
245	    )
246	    return 0
247	
248

250	    raise SystemExit(main())
